title: Immunefi bug bounty submission
description: One Immunefi submission. Mirrors the dashboard form fields per docs/research/IMMUNEFI_STRATEGY.md (verified 2026-06-08, 19/25 claims 3-0). Note: V2.3 severity is 4-tier (NOT V2.2's 5-tier with 'None'). Out-of-scope impacts cause rejection — impacts MUST be enumerated from the target program's bounty page.

program.description: Bounty program name (dropdown on dashboard). Must EXACTLY match the program's listed name — the form requires retyping it as redundant confirmation.

asset.description: Codebase URL selected from the program's Asset dropdown. 'Other' allowed but rejection-likely unless the finding is direct loss of funds.

impacts.description: One or more impacts from the program's enumerated 'Impacts In Scope'. NO custom impacts permitted. Severity is the HIGHEST selected impact's V2.3 tier.

severity.description: V2.3 4-tier severity. Derived from impacts but explicitly stated in Risk Breakdown.

title.description: Single-line summary of the vulnerability. Plumbline default: '<impact_phrase> via <FailureMode shape> in <contract>'.

bug_description.description: The 'Bug Description' field on the dashboard form. Two required subsections per Immunefi template.

brief_intro.description: 1-3 sentence executive summary of the bug.

details.description: Full technical analysis: affected code path, faulty assumption, attacker model. Markdown-rendered.

impact.description: What happens to users/protocol if exploited. Must map onto the program's in-scope impacts list (referenced in 'impacts' field). Markdown-rendered.

risk_breakdown.description: Severity rationalization. Should reference V2.3 4-tier classification and the smart-contract Critical impacts list (direct theft / NFT theft / permanent freeze / governance manipulation / manipulable RNG / unauthorized mint / unintended NFT alteration / protocol insolvency) where relevant.

recommendation.description: Concrete fix description. Pre-fix code → post-fix code form preferred. Markdown-rendered.

references.description: Markdown links + Immunefi-style citations. Past similar findings, EIP references, etc.

proof_of_concept.description: MUST be runnable exploit code. NOT prose, NOT screenshots, NOT a unit test, NOT the project's own contracts. Local mainnet fork only — touching mainnet/public testnet triggers permanent ban.

framework.description: PoC framework. Foundry recommended (canonical template repo at github.com/immunefi-team/forge-poc-templates). Hardhat also acceptable.

code.description: Full source code of the runnable PoC. Forge test file, Hardhat test file, or attack contract. Markdown-rendered with code fence on submission.

run_instructions.description: Exact CLI invocation that reproduces the exploit on a local mainnet fork. e.g. 'forge test --match-test test_exploit --fork-url $RPC --fork-block 19000000 -vvvv'.

fork_block.description: Block number for local fork (canonical: use a recent mainnet block where the vulnerable state holds).

plumbline_provenance.description: Internal plumbline tracking. NOT submitted to Immunefi.

v23_critical_impact.description: Which V2.3 smart-contract Critical impact this maps to.

examples.title: SignatureReplay in Vault.withdraw permits funds drain via re-used permit signature

bug_description.brief_intro: The withdraw() entry point lacks a nonce check on the EIP-2612 permit signature, allowing replay across blocks within the deadline window.

examples.impact: An attacker who observes a single permit signature can replay it to drain the victim's vault balance indefinitely until deadline.
examples.risk_breakdown: V2.3 Critical — direct theft of user funds — enumerated impact for Smart Contracts. No external preconditions required beyond observation of one permit tx on mainnet.
examples.recommendation: Add nonce increment to the permit validation path...
